verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin exists as a soluble plasma glycoprotein and as a multimeric form deposited in the extracellular matrix (ECM). Although it binds integrins and other cell-surface receptors, this binding is reversible non-covalent interaction — fibronectin is not itself a transmembrane protein, GPI-anchored, or stably tethered to a TM partner via wash-resistant linkage. The ECM/matrix-deposited form is covalently cross-linked into the stroma (transglutaminase) but this is matrix deposition, not cell-surface PM anchoring. Checking all 5 contextual buckets: no documented cancer/stress ecto-pool on the outer PM leaflet as a stably anchored protein; no narrow germline/developmental-lineage TM display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented minority PM-anchored pool via cycling; post-translational tethering is matrix deposition (excluded explicitly as secreted_only). The 'cell surface' mention in NCBI refers to ECM/pericellular matrix assembly, not TM anchoring. Anti-FN therapeutic programs target the soluble or ECM-deposited pool, not a cell-surface TM-anchored form.
